race.name: CRC 506 Gran Fondo Costa Rica
race.tagline: Costa Rica's UCI Gran Fondo qualifier on the stunning Pacific coast

vitals.location: Jacó, Garabito, Puntarenas Province, Costa Rica
vitals.entry_fee: Not specified; register at official site

climate.overview: Warm tropical (77-90°F), humid Pacific coast, June rain risk

terrain.overview: Flattish coastal road (Ruta 34) with ocean views, minimal climbs

logistics.explanation: The event's coastal route from Jacó to Quepos and back is well-defined with clear start and finish points at the Jacó Civic Center, but one rider review notes very poor organization, preventing a perfect score.[1][2][4]

length.explanation: The Gran Fondo offers a substantial 141 km distance along the Pacific coastline, ideal for endurance riders, complemented by a 70 km Medio Fondo option for broader participation.[1][2]

technicality.explanation: The flattish course primarily follows the same coastal road south to Quepos with minimal challenging features, making it straightforward for most riders.[2]

elevation.explanation: With only 841m of total elevation gain over 141 km on a mostly flat coastal path, the route lacks significant climbs, suiting beginners over mountaineers.[2]

climate.explanation: June's tropical Pacific coast weather in Jacó brings warm temperatures and potential humidity or rain, offering scenic riding but requiring heat acclimation.[1][3]

altitude.explanation: The sea-level route along Costa Rica's Pacific coastline stays at low elevation throughout, providing no high-altitude challenges or thin-air difficulties.[2]

adventure.explanation: Riding 141 km through Costa Rica's stunning beaches, national parks, and wildlife-rich coastal areas from Jacó to Quepos delivers a thrilling tropical exploration.[2][3]

prestige.explanation: As a UCI Gran Fondo World Series qualifier attracting 700 riders in past editions, it elevates Costa Rica's profile in international cycling but remains regionally focused.[1][2][5]

race_quality.explanation: The UCI-sanctioned event features age-graded categories for men and women across Gran and Medio Fondos, ensuring fair competition on a scenic, well-mapped course.[1][2]

experience.explanation: The paradisiacal Jacó backdrop with ocean views, post-ride beach relaxation, and Costa Rica's tourism allure create an unforgettable, immersive cycling adventure.[2][3]

community.explanation: With 700+ riders in prior editions and broad categories from 19-80+, it fosters a vibrant Central American cycling scene as the region's biggest mass-participation event.[1][2]

field_depth.explanation: Solid regional turnout with UCI qualifiers draws competitive local and international pelotons, though depth is moderate compared to European Gran Fondos.[1][2]

value.explanation: UCI prestige, stunning coastal scenery, and tourist perks like beaches and parks offer excellent bang-for-buck for a qualifier event in paradise.[2][3]

expenses.explanation: Travel to remote Jacó in Puntarenas adds costs for international visitors, but local accessibility and included race logistics keep it reasonable.[1][3]

ratings_explanations.logistics: Well-organized UCI event, accessible from SJO airport (1.5hr), limited to 1000 riders[1][2]
ratings_explanations.length: Legitimate 141km Gran Fondo distance with Medio option[1][2]
ratings_explanations.technicality: Flattish coastal road, low technical demands[2]
ratings_explanations.elevation: Only 841m total - flattest Gran Fondo profile[2]
ratings_explanations.climate: Warm tropical beauty but June wet season risks (2025 canceled)[9]
ratings_explanations.adventure: Stunning Pacific coast from Jacó to Quepos[1][2]
ratings_explanations.prestige: UCI Gran Fondo World Series qualifier (franchise prestige 3)[1][2]
ratings_explanations.race_quality: Official UCI medals, age-category qualification to Worlds[1][2]
ratings_explanations.experience: Ocean views, mass start from civic center, tourist paradise[2]
ratings_explanations.community: Central America's largest cycling event, strong local support[2]
ratings_explanations.value: UCI Worlds qualifier access in beautiful location[1][2]
ratings_explanations.expenses: Costa Rica travel costs but good event value[2]
ratings_explanations.cultural_impact: Costa Rica's premier cycling showcase to world[1]

biased_opinion.overview: Tropical paradise UCI qualifier with stunning coastal views but flattish profile and wet season risks. Perfect for speed-focused qualifiers seeking Worlds qualification in beach setting.

final_verdict.one_liner: Strong regional UCI event with international appeal and qualification prestige.

logistics.overview: Nearest airport: Juan Santamaría (SJO, ~1.5hr drive). Quepos domestic (XQP). Start/Finish: Centro Cívico por la Paz, Jacó. Medio start: Parque de Quepos. Limited 1000 spots[1][2]

history.overview: Founded 2023 as Costa Rica's first UCI Gran Fondo by RPMTV/Fecoci. 2023: 700 riders San José-Puntarenas. 2025: Jacó-Quepos route announced but canceled (tropical wave). 2026: June 7 edition[1][2][9]

course_description.character: 141.4km Gran Fondo: Neutral 2km start Jacó Civic Center, south on Ruta 34 coastal highway to Quepos loop/turnaround, return to Jacó (841m elevation, flattish). Medio Fondo (72.9km): Quepos start 8AM covering return leg to Jacó finish. **Suffering zones**: Heat/humidity, headwinds on exposed coast, potential rain[1][2]

guide_variables.signature_feature: Pacific Ocean views on flattish qualifier course

non_negotiables: UCI Gran Fondo World Series qualification (top 25% per age/gender)
Only Costa Rican event awarding official UCI medals
Mass start from Jacó Civic Center

youtube_data.search_query: CRC 506 Gran Fondo Costa Rica gran fondo cycling Jacó

videos.title: CRC Gran Fondo Costa Rica
videos.description:
¡Demostrá de qué estás hecho!  El CRC 506 Gran Fondo Costa Rica puede ser tu boleto directo al Campeonato Mundial de Ruta del UCI Gran Fondo World Series.  ¡Ganá tu lugar entre los mejores y pedaleá con fuerza para ganar una medalla oficial UCI!
🗓️ Fecha: 1 de junio 2025
📍 Ruta: Jacó - Quepos - Jacó
🔗 Inscribite ya en 👉 http://crc-506.com
Gran Fondo 141,4 km
Medio Fondo 69,8 km
🏅Se entregarán medallas UCI al 25% de los ciclistas de cada categoría que finalicen el fondo, que simboliza su clasificación para el Campeonato Mundial.
#CRC506 #GranFondoCostaRica #UCIGranFondo

videos.title: MANRIQUE MATA CRC 506 GRAN FONDO UCI
videos.description:
CRC 506 Gran Fondo de Costa Rica, cuya tercera edición se celebrará el domingo 1 de junio de 2025.

Una nueva y espectacular ruta que recorrerá Jacó – Quepos – Jacó. 
Habrá competencias de Gran Fondo con una distancia de 141.4 km para hombres y mujeres, y de Medio Fondo con un recorrido de 69.8 km también para hombres y mujeres.

El dedicado oficial del evento será Ministro del Deporte, Donald Rojas.

#gccomunicaciones #deporte #ciclismo #granfondo

rider_intel.search_text: The available transcript for the CRC 506 Gran Fondo Costa Rica contains minimal intelligible content, with only brief mentions of heat and music. No specific course details, terrain descriptions, or racing insights are provided in the transcript. The race is an 87.6-mile gravel event in Jacó, Costa Rica with 2,759 feet of elevation gain, but rider experiences and course characteristics cannot be extracted from the current transcript material.
